summary: Swapped the fancy progress fraction values in configure.py so the displayed code progress and data progress numbers align with their intended labels: code/Trophies uses 293 and data/Event Matches uses 51. This was a tiny repository configuration fix with no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: In configure.py under the Progress section, config.progress_code_fancy_frac changed from 51 to 293.
In configure.py under the Progress section, config.progress_data_fancy_frac changed from 293 to 51.
The associated fancy progress item labels were unchanged: code item remained "Trophies" and data item remained "Event Matches".
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal by only swapping the two incorrect numeric assignments, avoiding unrelated configuration churn.
Preserved the existing progress labels and only corrected the values associated with them, making the intent of the fix clear from the diff.
decomp_lessons: Progress-display configuration can be semantically important even when it does not affect matching; incorrect fancy progress numbers can mislead contributors about project status.
When maintaining repository progress metadata, verify that each numeric fraction is paired with the correct human-readable milestone label.
Small configure.py changes should stay tightly scoped because they affect global project reporting and are easy to audit when isolated.
naming_conventions: The progress config fields use the prefix config.progress_.
Fancy progress fields distinguish code and data with progress_code_fancy_* and progress_data_fancy_*.
The *_fancy_frac fields store numeric progress fractions, while *_fancy_item fields store the display label for the fancy progress item.
follow_up_queries: Look for later changes to configure.py progress_code_fancy_frac or progress_data_fancy_frac to see whether the fancy progress scheme changed again.
Search for documentation or scripts consuming progress_use_fancy to understand how these fraction values are displayed.
